response.justification: The candidate has a broad generalist background with genuine geospatial exposure (GISCollective, Green Map, FOSS4G talk) and some C/C++ history, but the core requirement — modern, production C++ in a routing or navigation pipeline context — is not met. C++ does not appear in the candidate's listed skills, their most recent C/C++ work is from an early LabWindows/CVI role and a bachelor thesis project, and the past several years have been spent almost exclusively in TypeScript, Node.js, and web/mobile stacks. The geospatial work is meaningful but oriented toward data collection rather than routing tile generation, traffic integration, or pipeline latency optimization.
strengths: Founded and maintains a geospatial open-source platform (GISCollective) with real-world usage and a FOSS4G conference presentation, showing genuine domain interest
Demonstrated exposure to high-throughput, low-latency service design (millions of HTTP requests/second with DLang async I/O and caching)
Long career with consistent Linux usage across server-side, embedded, and DevOps contexts
concerns: C++ is absent from the candidate's listed skills and all substantive C/C++ experience appears dated (LabWindows role and a bachelor thesis project)
No experience with routing tile pipelines, navigation data transformation, or real-time traffic integration — the central technical domain of the role
Career trajectory over recent years is firmly web/TypeScript-focused, making a pivot to systems-level C++ routing infrastructure a very large technical gap
